opening_message: I need a Miami trip from New York/JFK from June 22 to June 25 with flights, a hotel, and a rental car. My total budget is $1,050 and I do not want the absolute cheapest trip if a slightly better full package still fits.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want a full Miami package from New York/JFK but not in a purely cheapest-wins way. If a somewhat better hotel or car still fits the full budget, you want the agent to compare complete bundles instead of locking onto the lowest-price component in each category automatically.
known_info: You need JFK-MIA round-trip flights, a Miami hotel, and a Miami car rental.
unknown_info: Which full bundle is the best value while still fitting budget
task_rules: Ask the agent to compare full-trip bundles, not just the cheapest item in each category.
Use JFK as the New York origin and MIA as the Miami airport; do not introduce a different origin or airport unless the agent asks you to clarify an ambiguity.
If a slightly better hotel or car still fits the total budget, prefer the better hotel first; keep the cheaper car unless the car upgrade also fits after the better hotel is included.
End with [TASK_DONE] once the chosen flight, hotel, and car are all booked and the total is explained.
For new booking creation, booking-stage preferences should match your profile preferences unless task-specific rules say otherwise.
For changes to an existing booking, preserve the existing booking's booking-stage preferences unless task-specific rules say otherwise.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
For a new booking, including a replacement after canceling an existing booking, if the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer using your profile preferences.

For a flight change to an existing booking, if the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, say to keep the existing booking's preferences unless the task-specific rules say otherwise.

If the agent creates or changes a booking with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not try to verify raw flight_id. Budget remains a hard constraint.

Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking has the expected booking-stage preferences for that operation: profile preferences for new bookings, existing persisted preferences for changes.

task_summary:
Traveler wants a full-trip Miami package under a mid-range budget, but prefers the best overall bundle within budget rather than the absolute cheapest component in every category. Correct resolution: the agent should compare whole-package tradeoffs and select the higher-value hotel while keeping the total trip under budget with the cheaper car.

Challenge: This tests bundle-level optimization instead of greedy per-component minimization.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must compare at least two complete flight-plus-hotel-plus-car bundles rather than optimizing components in isolation.
Agent must make clear why choosing hotel HOTEL-ET03B with car CAR-ET03A is the best in-budget bundle rather than simply defaulting to the cheapest hotel and cheapest car.
